steps.title: Make two Desmos rows
steps.content: The line passes through $(0,7)$. A slope of $\frac{1}{3}$ means moving $3$ units right raises $y$ by $1$, so a second point is $(3,8)$.

steps.title: Run linear regression
steps.content: Enter $y_1 \sim mx_1+b$. Desmos gives $m=0.333333...$ and $b=7$, so the equation is $f(x)=\frac{1}{3}x+7$.

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: The regression equation matches choice A. The other three choices keep the same slope form but use the wrong $y$-intercept, so they fail the point $(0,7)$. Choice A is correct.
